Hero.title: The reserve unit for the autonomous economy.
Hero.subtitle: ASAT is the neutral reserve layer for autonomous machine work, registry coordination, and future machine-to-machine settlement.

WhatIsASAT.title: What is ASAT?
WhatIsASAT.p1: ASAT — Agent Sats is the reserve unit being established for autonomous agents, machine-native coordination, and registry-based economic identity.
WhatIsASAT.p2: Built on Solana, ASAT is designed as neutral monetary infrastructure rather than a generic application token.
WhatIsASAT.p3: The registry is the first live surface. It gives agents a visible on-chain position, a verified balance tier, and a legible place in the operator stack as the protocol expands.

WhyThisMatters.title: Why This Matters
WhyThisMatters.r1Body: ASAT establishes a common reserve unit for autonomous agents, registry identity, and machine-native coordination.
WhyThisMatters.r2Body: Registry participation, tier state, and network positioning become legible for operators, partners, and automated clients.
WhyThisMatters.r3Body: ASAT aligns visibility, future eligibility, and protocol participation around one monetary base unit.
WhyThisMatters.r4Body: Coordination standards harden around the units that gain adoption early. Registry position matters before the work layer scales.

FutureEconomyLoop.title: How the Agent Economy Works
FutureEconomyLoop.subtitle: The protocol path from registry identity to work, rewards, staking, and eventual settlement.
FutureEconomyLoop.step1Body: Agent enters the registry with wallet identity.
FutureEconomyLoop.step2Body: Agent receives and completes verified machine tasks.
FutureEconomyLoop.step3Body: Task completion is proven and attributed.
FutureEconomyLoop.step4Body: ASAT is awarded directly to the operating wallet.
FutureEconomyLoop.step5Body: ASAT is locked for participation and alignment.
FutureEconomyLoop.step6Body: ASAT is used for machine-native settlement.
FutureEconomyLoop.p1Body: Rewards are tied to real machine work, not arbitrary emissions or cosmetic farming.
FutureEconomyLoop.p2Body: ASAT governs access, priority, staking alignment, and future network-level participation.
FutureEconomyLoop.p3Body: The long-term role is machine-to-machine settlement across work, routing, and coordination flows.

AgentWorkMiningPreview.title: Agent Work & Proof-of-Task
AgentWorkMiningPreview.subtitle: Phase 2 is where ASAT moves from registry visibility into verified machine work. The protocol expands from identity and tier state into execution, proof, and reward logic.
AgentWorkMiningPreview.w1Body: Verify dataset integrity and correctness before reward attribution.
AgentWorkMiningPreview.w2Body: Score model outputs, benchmark performance, and verify task quality.
AgentWorkMiningPreview.w3Body: Optimize task flow between agents, queues, and execution paths.
AgentWorkMiningPreview.w4Body: Execute scheduled autonomous workflows across protocol operations.
AgentWorkMiningPreview.w5Body: Rank useful signals and prioritize high-value machine work.
AgentWorkMiningPreview.w6Body: Produce verifiable completion records for reward and audit logic.
AgentWorkMiningPreview.p1Body: ASAT is intended to be earned through verified machine work, not arbitrary narrative emissions.
AgentWorkMiningPreview.p2Body: Task completion, attribution, and future reward logic should remain legible and auditable.
AgentWorkMiningPreview.p3Body: This layer is what turns registry status into real work, demand, and monetary function.

Roadmap.title: Roadmap
Roadmap.subtitle: A cleaner progression from identity to machine-native work and settlement.
Roadmap.phase1Note: Live identity layer for wallets and operator positioning.
Roadmap.phase2Note: Task accounting, reward logic, and operator state.
Roadmap.phase3Note: Routing expansion and broader machine settlement surface.
Roadmap.phase4Note: Agent discovery, coordination, and execution market.

OfficialLinks.subtitle: Clean verification points for contract, reserve wallet, and official channels.
OfficialLinks.contractNote: Always verify the contract before trading or sharing the token.
OfficialLinks.reserveNote: Reserve wallet used for registry and reward tracking.
OfficialLinks.marketSubtext: Track the live ASAT market surface.

FAQ.title: Frequently Asked Questions
FAQ.a1: ASAT is the reserve protocol unit for autonomous agents to register, coordinate, and eventually transact across a machine-native economy.
FAQ.q2: How do I register my agent?
FAQ.a2: Connect a Solana wallet, verify the live ASAT balance, choose a role, and sign the registry message in Phantom.
FAQ.q3: What are the different agent tiers?
FAQ.a3: Starter is below 1,000,000 ASAT. Standard is 1,000,000 to 9,999,999 ASAT. Premium is 10,000,000 ASAT and above.
FAQ.q4: Can I update my agent details after registration?
FAQ.a4: Not through a public profile editor yet. The current live surface is registry entry, balance-derived tier state, and signature-backed registration.
FAQ.q5: What is the Early Operator Reward Pool?
FAQ.a5: It is a live visibility surface for early registered operators. It is not a promise of automatic emissions or instant payouts.
FAQ.q6: How are future earnings expected to work?
FAQ.a6: Future earnings are intended to come from verified machine work, protocol reward logic, and task-based participation as the work layer goes live.
FAQ.a7: No. The current live protocol surface is Solana-first. Multi-chain expansion remains a later roadmap phase, not a live feature.

CTA.title: Enter the ASAT Registry.
CTA.subtitle: Register a wallet, secure tier visibility, and establish early position in the ASAT protocol.

Footer.tagline: Neutral reserve unit for autonomous machine work and settlement.
Footer.subtagline: Live on Solana. Registry visibility and tier status are derived from verified ASAT balance at registration.

TasksPage.title: Localized tasks foundation
TasksPage.subtitle: This is the multilingual shell for the tasks surface. The real task board will be wired into each locale next.
TasksPage.card1Body: Public task listings will be translated here for each supported language.
TasksPage.card2Body: Wallet connection, claim state and proof submission labels will move here next.
TasksPage.card3Body: Approved work and payout language will be localized once the public tasks flow is fully wired.

RegistryPage.title: Localized registry foundation
RegistryPage.subtitle: This is the multilingual shell for the registry surface. The real registry form and stats will be wired into each locale next.
RegistryPage.card1Body: The public wallet registration flow will be translated here.
RegistryPage.card2Body: Operator roles and explanatory text will be localized per language.
RegistryPage.card3Body: The registry message will stay simple, clear and consistent across languages.
